# Overview [CURRENT] — package: overview — rendered: 2026-04-14T20:16:44Z

Persistence operates in two tiers: an in-memory tier (session-scoped, low-latency, used for live queries) and a JPA/PostgreSQL tier (durable, artifact-scoped, used for long-term storage and replay). The in-memory tier drives all real-time operations — SSE replay, interrupt lookups, graph traversal. The JPA tier kicks in only when an execution completes and the artifact tree is flushed.

# Two-Tier Architecture [CURRENT] — package: two-tier-architecture — rendered: 2026-04-14T20:16:44Z

References: 10 files tracked (package: two-tier-architecture) — see `$.sections["Two-Tier Architecture"]` in .metadata/HEAD

```
In-Memory Tier (session-scoped)                    JPA/PostgreSQL Tier (durable)
─────────────────────────────────                   ────────────────────────────
InMemoryGraphRepository                             ArtifactEntity (table: artifact)
  ConcurrentHashMap<nodeId, GraphNode>                artifactKey, parentKey, executionKey
  findSubtree(), findInterruptByOrigin()              contentHash (SHA-256), contentJson (TEXT)
                                                      depth, templateStaticId, shared, childIds
InMemoryEventStreamRepository
  ConcurrentHashMap<nodeId, List<GraphEvent>>       SemanticRepresentationEntity
  + byId map for direct lookup                        targetArtifactKey, embeddings (binary)
  ArtifactKey.isDescendantOf() for scoped queries     summaries (JSON), classifications

InMemoryWorktreeRepository                          ArtifactRepository (Spring Data JPA + QueryDSL)
InMemoryRequestContextRepository                      findByContentHash(), findByKeyPrefix()
InMemoryRunPersistenceCheckRepository                 findByExecutionKeyAndArtifactType()
                                                      findAllExecutions(), findExecutionKeysBetween()
```

# Artifact Persistence Flow [CURRENT] — package: artifact-persistence-flow — rendered: 2026-04-14T20:16:44Z

References: 7 files tracked (package: artifact-persistence-flow) — see `$.sections["Artifact Persistence Flow"]` in .metadata/HEAD

```
Agent emits artifact (ArtifactEmissionService)
 │
 ▼
ArtifactEventListener.onEvent()
 │
 ├─ ArtifactEvent → walks tree by depth, inserts into in-memory ArtifactTreeBuilder
 ├─ Semantic graph events → mapped to EventArtifact via EventArtifactMapper
 └─ Stream events → mapped to MessageStreamArtifact
 │
 │  (artifacts may arrive before their parent — queued in pendingArtifactsByExecution)
 │
 ▼
ExecutionScopeService.completeExecution(workflowRunId, status)
 │
 ▼
ArtifactEventListener.finishPersistRemove(executionKey)
 │
 ▼
ArtifactService.doPersist()
 ├─ Collects all artifacts from the tree
 ├─ Groups by ArtifactKey (handles key collisions by creating child keys)
 ├─ Groups by contentHash:
 │    ├─ Hash exists in DB → creates DbRef (reference artifact, no payload duplication)
 │    └─ Templated content → creates TemplateDbRef instead of ArtifactDbRef
 ├─ Saves via ArtifactRepository (JPA)
 └─ Errors publish NodeErrorEvent to EventBus
```

# ArtifactKey: The Hierarchical Identifier [CURRENT] — package: artifactkey-the-hierarchical-identifier — rendered: 2026-04-14T20:16:44Z

`ArtifactKey` is a hierarchical `ak:ulid/ulid/...` string. Parent-child relationships are encoded in the key structure itself — `ArtifactKey.isDescendantOf()` checks if one key is a prefix of another. This enables efficient scoped queries without joins: all events/interrupts/permissions under a workflow run share a common key prefix.

# SemanticRepresentation: Post-Hoc Enrichment [CURRENT] — package: semanticrepresentation-post-hoc-enrichment — rendered: 2026-04-14T20:16:44Z

`SemanticRepresentationEntity` stores post-hoc semantic interpretations (embeddings, summaries, classifications, named entities) keyed by `targetArtifactKey`. It never mutates the source artifact — it's a separate table that enriches artifacts after the fact.

# Direct Persistence Bypass [CURRENT] — package: direct-persistence-bypass — rendered: 2026-04-14T20:16:44Z

References: 2 files tracked (package: direct-persistence-bypass) — see `$.sections["Direct Persistence Bypass"]` in .metadata/HEAD

If no execution root is present in memory but the artifact's parent exists in the persistent store (`artifactService.existsByArtifactKey()`), the artifact is persisted directly without being added to the in-memory tree. This prevents stale in-memory state from blocking persistence of late-arriving artifacts.
